ogTitle: Acacia Hills vs Alawa: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Acacia Hills and Alawa.
heading: Acacia Hills vs Alawa — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Acacia Hills, Northern Territory and Alawa, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Alawa has a lower overall climate risk score (23/100) compared to Acacia Hills (30/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Acacia Hills or Alawa safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Acacia Hills has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Alawa scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Acacia Hills scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Alawa scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Acacia Hills or Alawa?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Alawa has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
